question_text: A hydraulic system uses two cylinders with different cross-sectional areas. Cylinder A has an area of 0.5 m² and cylinder B has an area of 0.02 m². A force of 500 N is applied to cylinder A. Assuming the system is ideal and no energy is lost, which statement correctly compares the pressure and force in both cylinders?

options.label: A
options.text: The pressure in both cylinders is equal at 1000 Pa, and the force in cylinder B is 20 N

options.label: B
options.text: The pressure in both cylinders is equal at 1000 Pa, and the force in cylinder B is 20,000 N

options.label: C
options.text: The pressure in cylinder B is greater than in cylinder A, and the force in cylinder B is 12,500 N

options.label: D
options.text: The pressure in both cylinders is equal at 250 Pa, and the force in cylinder B is 5 N

explanation: In a hydraulic system, pressure is transmitted equally throughout the fluid. Pressure in cylinder A = Force ÷ Area = 500 N ÷ 0.5 m² = 1000 Pa. Since pressure is equal in both cylinders, pressure in cylinder B = 1000 Pa. Using P = F/A rearranged to F = P × A: Force in cylinder B = 1000 Pa × 0.02 m² = 20,000 N. This demonstrates the mechanical advantage of hydraulic systems.
